File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|山鉾巡行。春日神社前。 ==社殿== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|翁奉納（2006.1.1）]] :篠山藩主13代藩主{{ill|Aoyama Tadayoshi|de|Aoyama Tadayoshi|ja|青山忠良}}によって{{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期}}である{{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年}}（{{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久}}元年）に建てられた能舞台。忠良は{{ill|Noh|de|Nō|ja|能楽}}愛好家としても知られ、{{ill|Hōnō|de|Weihgabe|ja|奉納}}したものである。建築は稲山嘉七、永井理兵衛、舞台背景に描かれた{{ill|Pine (art)|de|Kiefer (Malerei)|ja|松}}の絵は{{ill|Matsuoka Sōemon|de|Matsuoka Sōemon|ja|松岡曾右衛門}}によるもので、当時{{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根}}以西において最も立派なものと称えられた。床板を踏む音を反響させるため、床下には大甕が伏せられているが、その完璧さは全国の能舞台中でも屈指といわれる。{{ill|New Year in Japan|de|Neujahr in Japan|ja|正月}}の{{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}}には、{{ill|Okina (Noh)|de|Okina (Nō)|ja|翁}}{{ill|Hōnō|de|Weihgabe|ja|奉納}}および毎年、{{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜}}の咲く{{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月}}上旬に「篠山春日能」が催される。{{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年}}（{{ill|Heisei|de|Heisei|ja|平成}}15年）には{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}に指定される篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板。 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂}} :{{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年}}（{{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和}}27年）8月に平和記念として建立された。内部の馬絵は、当初は旧拝殿内にあったもので、{{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安}}2年に{{ill|Matsudaira Tadakuni (Akashi Domain)|de|Matsudaira Tadakuni|ja|松平忠国 (播磨国明石藩主)}}が奉納した{{ill|Kano Naonobu|de|Kano Naonobu|ja|狩野尚信}}作の黒神馬は精緻なもので価値が高く、この絵馬だけには金網が張ってあった。これは、夜な夜な絵から馬が抜け出し黒岡の田畑の豆を食い荒らしたという伝説があるためである。この黒神馬は多紀名宝にも指定されている。 == Cultural Properties == ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * 能舞台 (Noh stage) ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * 黒神馬 (Black Divine Horse painting) ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} 翁奉納。午前0時過ぎより行われる。 * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} 春日能。 * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} 秋の祭礼。 ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR福知山線「{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}」 * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: ウイング神姫（旧神姫グリーンバス）「春日神社前」より徒歩すぐ。 ==Notes== ==See also== * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (deity)|de|Kasuga (Gottheit)|ja|春日神}} ==External links== *{{Official website|http://kasuga.tanba-sasayama.net/|丹波篠山 春日神社}} *[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O1fzbIBsys4 春日神社秋祭り（篠山市）宵宮　2008.10.18　～丹波新報社]{{ill|Dead link|de|Toter Link|ja|リンク切れ}}|date=2020年1月 *[https://tourism.sasayama.jp/association/ 丹波篠山観光協会 公式サイト] *[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?hl=ja&lr&um=1&ie=UTF-8&q=%E6%98%A5%E6%97%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&near=%E5%85%B5%E5%BA%AB%E7%9C%8C%E7%AF%A0%E5%B1%B1%E5%B8%82&fb=1&view=text&latlng=15104569364938192884 春日神社地図] *[http://edu.city.sasayama.hyogo.jp/tiikibunka/kasuganou34/kasuganou34.html 第46回春日能（兵庫県篠山市社会教育課・文化財課 ）] {{ill|Shinto footer|de|Shintō-Fußzeile|ja|神道 横}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (structures)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tamba-Sasayama]] [[Category:History of Tamba-Sasayama]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:春日神社 (丹波篠山市)]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein (Tamba-Sasayama)]]--> [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]]

{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}
{{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|姫大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}} | main_hall = | main_festival = 3rd Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} | image = [[File:篠山春日神社本殿sasayama-kasugajinjaPA156109.jpg|200px|Main Hall]] | map = {{Location map | Japan Hyogo | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 04 | lat_sec = 41.7 | lon_deg = 135 | lon_min = 13 | lon_sec = 07.8 | label = Kasuga Jinja | position = center | width = 200 | float = center }} }} '''Kasuga Jinja''' (春日神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75. It was {{ill|Branch shrine|de|Zweigschrein|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in {{ill|Nara|de|Nara|ja|奈良}}. Under the {{ill|Former shrine ranking system|de|Ehemaliges Shintō-Rangsystem|ja|旧社格}}, it was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}}. ==Overview== 奈良の{{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}}の分祠を祭り、地元では「おかすがさん」と呼ばれる{{ill|Ujigami|de|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}。{{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}}で演じられる春の{{ill|Kasuga Noh|de|Kasuga Nō|ja|春日能}}と{{ill|Autumn|de|Herbst|ja|秋}}の{{ill|Festival|de|Fest|ja|祭礼}}（{{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}}第3土・日）の、{{ill|Kyoto|de|Kyōto|ja|京都}}の {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|de|Yasaka-Schrein|ja|八坂神社|lt=Yasaka|lt_ja=八坂|lt_de=Yasaka}} の{{ill|Gion Festival|de|Gion-Fest|ja|祇園祭}}を模したといわれる4基の{{ill|Mikoshi|de|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}、華麗な飾り付けを施された9基の{{ill|Yamahoko|de|Yamahoko|ja|山鉾}}巡行は京文化を強くうかがわせ、{{ill|Fue|de|Fue|ja|笛}}や{{ill|Kane (instrument)|de|Kane (Musikinstrument)|ja|鐘}}の囃子を響かせながら{{ill|Little Kyoto|de|Klein-Kyoto|ja|小京都}}の雰囲気が残る古い町並みを練り歩く様子は、近代的な町で行われる本場の京都の祇園祭よりも情緒があるものとして知られる。 ==History== {{ill|Heian period|de|Heian-Zeit|ja|平安時代}}初期の{{ill|876|de|876|ja|876年}}（{{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|貞観}}18年）当時、日置荘の領有者であった{{ill|Fujiwara no Mototsune|de|Fujiwara no Mototsune|ja|藤原基経}}、{{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|de|Fujiwara no Tokihira|ja|藤原時平}} {{ill|Parent and child|de|Eltern und Kind|ja|親子|lt=Father and Son|lt_ja=父子|lt_de=Vater und Sohn}} により、藤原氏の氏神である{{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}}（{{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県}}）より分祀し、黒岡村南部の当時は笹山と呼ばれた現在は{{ill|Sasayama Castle|de|Burg File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|山鉾巡行。春日神社前。 ==社殿== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|翁奉納（2006.1.1）]] :篠山藩主13代藩主{{ill|Aoyama Tadayoshi|de|Aoyama Tadayoshi|ja|青山忠良}}によって{{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期}}である{{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年}}（{{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久}}元年）に建てられた能舞台。忠良は{{ill|Noh|de|Nō|ja|能楽}}愛好家としても知られ、{{ill|Hōnō|de|Weihgabe|ja|奉納}}したものである。建築は稲山嘉七、永井理兵衛、舞台背景に描かれた{{ill|Pine (art)|de|Kiefer (Malerei)|ja|松}}の絵は{{ill|Matsuoka Sōemon|de|Matsuoka Sōemon|ja|松岡曾右衛門}}によるもので、当時{{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根}}以西において最も立派なものと称えられた。床板を踏む音を反響させるため、床下には大甕が伏せられているが、その完璧さは全国の能舞台中でも屈指といわれる。{{ill|New Year in Japan|de|Neujahr in Japan|ja|正月}}の{{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}}には、{{ill|Okina (Noh)|de|Okina (Nō)|ja|翁}}{{ill|Hōnō|de|Weihgabe|ja|奉納}}および毎年、{{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜}}の咲く{{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月}}上旬に「篠山春日能」が催される。{{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年}}（{{ill|Heisei|de|Heisei|ja|平成}}15年）には{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}に指定される篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板。 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂}} :{{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年}}（{{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和}}27年）8月に平和記念として建立された。内部の馬絵は、当初は旧拝殿内にあったもので、{{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安}}2年に{{ill|Matsudaira Tadakuni (Akashi Domain)|de|Matsudaira Tadakuni|ja|松平忠国 (播磨国明石藩主)}}が奉納した{{ill|Kano Naonobu|de|Kano Naonobu|ja|狩野尚信}}作の黒神馬は精緻なもので価値が高く、この絵馬だけには金網が張ってあった。これは、夜な夜な絵から馬が抜け出し黒岡の田畑の豆を食い荒らしたという伝説があるためである。この黒神馬は多紀名宝にも指定されている。 == Cultural Properties == ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * 能舞台 (Noh stage) ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * 黒神馬 (Black Divine Horse painting) ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} 翁奉納。午前0時過ぎより行われる。 * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} 春日能。 * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} 秋の祭礼。 ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR福知山線「{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}」 * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: ウイング神姫（旧神姫グリーンバス）「春日神社前」より徒歩すぐ。 ==Notes== ==See also== * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (deity)|de|Kasuga (Gottheit)|ja|春日神}} ==External links== *{{Official website|http://kasuga.tanba-sasayama.net/|丹波篠山 春日神社}} *[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O1fzbIBsys4 春日神社秋祭り（篠山市）宵宮　2008.10.18　～丹波新報社]{{ill|Dead link|de|Toter Link|ja|リンク切れ}}|date=2020年1月 *[https://tourism.sasayama.jp/association/ 丹波篠山観光協会 公式サイト] *[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?hl=ja&lr&um=1&ie=UTF-8&q=%E6%98%A5%E6%97%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&near=%E5%85%B5%E5%BA%AB%E7%9C%8C%E7%AF%A0%E5%B1%B1%E5%B8%82&fb=1&view=text&latlng=15104569364938192884 春日神社地図] *[http://edu.city.sasayama.hyogo.jp/tiikibunka/kasuganou34/kasuganou34.html 第46回春日能（兵庫県篠山市社会教育課・文化財課 ）] {{ill|Shinto footer|de|Shintō-Fußzeile|ja|神道 横}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (structures)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tamba-Sasayama]] [[Category:History of Tamba-Sasayama]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:春日神社 (丹波篠山市)]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein (Tamba-Sasayama)]]--> [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]]

(%EB%8B%A8%EB%B0%94%EC%82%AC%EC%82%AC%EC%95%BC%EB%A7%88%EC%8B%9C)]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Jinja (春日神社) | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|04|41.7|N|135|13|07.8|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Jinja (Tamba-Sasayama)}} | enshrined_deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}
{{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|姫大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}} | main_hall = | main_festival = 3rd Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} | image = [[File:篠山春日神社本殿sasayama-kasugajinjaPA156109.jpg|200px|Main Hall]] | map = {{Location map | Japan Hyogo | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 04 | lat_sec = 41.7 | lon_deg = 135 | lon_min = 13 | lon_sec = 07.8 | label = Kasuga Jinja | position = center | width = 200 | float = center }} }} '''Kasuga Jinja''' (春日神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75. It was {{ill|Branch shrine|de|Zweigschrein|ja|分霊}} from the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in {{ill|Nara|de|Nara|ja|奈良}}. Under the {{ill|Former shrine ranking system|de|Ehemaliges Shintō-Rangsystem|ja|旧社格}}, it was a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}}. ==Overview== 奈良の{{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}}の分祠を祭り、地元では「おかすがさん」と呼ばれる{{ill|Ujigami|de|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}。{{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}}で演じられる春の{{ill|Kasuga Noh|de|Kasuga Nō|ja|春日能}}と{{ill|Autumn|de|Herbst|ja|秋}}の{{ill|Festival|de|Fest|ja|祭礼}}（{{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}}第3土・日）の、{{ill|Kyoto|de|Kyōto|ja|京都}}の {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|de|Yasaka-Schrein|ja|八坂神社|lt=Yasaka|lt_ja=八坂|lt_de=Yasaka}} の{{ill|Gion Festival|de|Gion-Fest|ja|祇園祭}}を模したといわれる4基の{{ill|Mikoshi|de|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}、華麗な飾り付けを施された9基の{{ill|Yamahoko|de|Yamahoko|ja|山鉾}}巡行は京文化を強くうかがわせ、{{ill|Fue|de|Fue|ja|笛}}や{{ill|Kane (instrument)|de|Kane (Musikinstrument)|ja|鐘}}の囃子を響かせながら{{ill|Little Kyoto|de|Klein-Kyoto|ja|小京都}}の雰囲気が残る古い町並みを練り歩く様子は、近代的な町で行われる本場の京都の祇園祭よりも情緒があるものとして知られる。 ==History== {{ill|Heian period|de|Heian-Zeit|ja|平安時代}}初期の{{ill|876|de|876|ja|876年}}（{{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|貞観}}18年）当時、日置荘の領有者であった{{ill|Fujiwara no Mototsune|de|Fujiwara no Mototsune|ja|藤原基経}}、{{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|de|Fujiwara no Tokihira|ja|藤原時平}} {{ill|Parent and child|de|Eltern und Kind|ja|親子|lt=Father and Son|lt_ja=父子|lt_de=Vater und Sohn}} により、藤原氏の氏神である{{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}}（{{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県}}）より分祀し、黒岡村南部の当時は笹山と呼ばれた現在は{{ill|Sasayama Castle|de|Burg Sasayama|ja|篠山城}}のある小山に勧請し、祀り氏神としたのが起源とされる。後の{{ill|1609|de|1609|ja|1609年}}（{{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長}}14年）、篠山城築城の際に現在の地に遷された。 秋の祭礼に使われる4柱の{{ill|Shinrei|de|Shinrei|ja|神霊}}を移した金神興は、2基を黒岡村より{{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}}7年{{ill|June|de|Juni|ja|6月}}に、他の2基は{{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}}8年{{ill|September|de|September|ja|9月}}（{{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}}13年の説もある）篠山町より寄進されたもの。9台のちんちき山と呼ばれる山鉾は{{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}}3年以降に各町内より寄進されたもので、京都の祇園の山鉾を模して造られており、絢爛豪華である。{{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治}}後期までは鳳凰・孔雀・蘇鉄・猩猩等の{{ill|Hoko (festival float)|de|Hoko|ja|鉾}}を付け巡行したが、{{ill|Electric wires|de|Überlandleitungen|ja|電線}}が障害となり現在は鉾を付けずに巡行される。また{{ill|Taiko drum float|de|Taiko-Festwagen|ja|太鼓御興}}は、{{ill|Culture and Bunsei eras|de|Bunka/Bunsei|ja|文化・文政}}のころ、{{ill|Ōjiyama ware|de|Ōjiyama-Keramik|ja|王地山焼}}の{{ill|Potter (ceramics)|de|Töpfer|ja|陶工}}たちが担ぎ始めたのが由来と伝えられる。 ;鉾山 [[File:春日神社山鉾巡行PA160707.jpg|thumbnail|180px|鉾山巡行 蘇鉄山（魚屋町）]] :長さ6メートル、高さ約5メートル、{{ill|Roof|de|Dach|ja|屋根}}までの高さは京都祇園祭・山鉾の約三分の二ほどの規模ながら京都の祇園祭に強い影響を受けている。初期の鉾山は現在よりも小形で簡素なものだったが、{{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代}}中期頃に現在の規模となり華麗さを増した。初めて鉾山が作られたのは{{ill|1663|de|1663|ja|1663年}}（{{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}}3年）の三笠山（上河原町）と伝えられる。{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama municipal designation|de|Städtisches Kulturgut Tamba-Sasayama|ja|篠山市指定文化財}}。 *上町4台 :三笠山（上河原町）、鳳凰山（下河原町）、孔雀山（上立町）、高砂山（下立町） *下町5台 :剣鉾山（呉服町）、猩々山（上二階町）、諫鼓山（下二階町）、蘇鉄山（魚屋町）、鏡山（西町） ;神輿 :{{ill|1694|de|1694|ja|1694年}}（{{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}}7年）および{{ill|1779|de|1779|ja|1779年}}（{{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}}8年）に寄進奉納されたが、4基あるのは春日神社の4祭神にちなんだもので、4祭神とは{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}}、{{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}、{{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}、{{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}}である。 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} 翁奉納。午前0時過ぎより行われる。 * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} 春日能。 * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} 秋の祭礼。 ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR福知山線「{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}」 * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: ウイング神姫（旧神姫グリーンバス）「春日神社前」より徒歩すぐ。 ==Notes== ==See also== * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (deity)|de|Kasuga (Gottheit)|ja|春日神}} ==External links== *{{Official website|http://kasuga.tanba-sasayama.net/|丹波篠山 春日神社}} *[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O1fzbIBsys4 春日神社秋祭り（篠山市）宵宮　2008.10.18　～丹波新報社]{{ill|Dead link|de|Toter Link|ja|リンク切れ}}|date=2020年1月 *[https://tourism.sasayama.jp/association/ 丹波篠山観光協会 公式サイト] *[https://www.google.co.jp/maps?hl=ja&lr&um=1&ie=UTF-8&q=%E6%98%A5%E6%97%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&near=%E5%85%B5%E5%BA%AB%E7%9C%8C%E7%AF%A0%E5%B1%B1%E5%B8%82&fb=1&view=text&latlng=15104569364938192884 春日神社地図] *[http://edu.city.sasayama.hyogo.jp/tiikibunka/kasuganou34/kasuganou34.html 第46回春日能（兵庫県篠山市社会教育課・文化財課 ）] {{ill|Shinto footer|de|Shintō-Fußzeile|ja|神道 横}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Kasuga shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (structures)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tamba-Sasayama]] [[Category:History of Tamba-Sasayama]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[ja:春日神社 (丹波篠山市)]] [[de:Kasuga-Schrein (Tamba-Sasayama)]]--> [[Category:Shrine pages to be written]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Jinja (春日神社) | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|04|41.7|N|135|13|07.8|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Jinja (Tamba-Sasayama)}} | enshrined_deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}
{{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|姫大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}} | main_hall = | main_festival = 3rd Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} | image = [[File:篠山春日神社本殿sasayama-kasugajinjaPA156109.jpg|200px|Main Hall]] | map = {{Location map | Japan Hyogo | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 04 | lat_sec = 41.7 | lon_deg = 135 | lon_min = 13 | lon_sec = 07.8 | label = Kasuga Jinja | position = center | width = 200 | float = center }} }} '''Kasuga Jinja''' (春日神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75. It was {{ill|Branch shrine|de|Zweigschrein|ja|分霊}} of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in {{ill|Nara|de|Nara|ja|奈良}}. Under the {{ill|Former shrine ranking system|de|Ehemaliges Shintō-Rangsystem|ja|旧社格}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}}. ==Overview== It enshrines a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} of Nara, and locally the deity is known as "Okasuga-san" – an {{ill|Ujigami|de|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}. The spring {{ill|Kasuga Noh|de|Kasuga Nō|ja|春日能}} performed on the {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} and the autumn {{ill|Festival|de|Fest|ja|祭礼}} held on the third Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} are said to be modeled after the {{ill|Gion Festival|de|Gion-Fest|ja|祇園祭}} of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|de|Yasaka-Schrein|ja|八坂神社|lt=Yasaka|lt_ja=八坂|lt_de=Yasaka}} in Kyoto. In this celebration, four mikoshi (portable shrines) and nine elaborately decorated yamahoko floats parade through an old town that retains the atmosphere of "Little Kyoto" with the resonant sounds of flutes ({{ill|Fue|de|Fue|ja|笛}}) and bells ({{ill|Kane (instrument)|de|Kane (Musikinstrument)|ja|鐘}}), evoking a charm regarded as more atmospheric than the modern rendition of Kyoto’s Gion Festival. ==History== In the early {{ill|Heian period|de|Heian-Zeit|ja|平安時代}}, in {{ill|876|de|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 18), Fujiwara no Mototsune and his son Fujiwara no Tokihira, then lords of Hioki Manor, enshrined a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} (located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県}}) – the guardian deity of the Fujiwara clan – in a small hill in the southern part of Kurooka Village (then known as Sasayama, where {{ill|Sasayama Castle|de|Burg Sasayama|ja|篠山城}} now stands). This is considered the origin of the shrine. Later, in {{ill|1609|de|1609|ja|1609年}} ({{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14), during the construction of Sasayama Castle, the shrine was relocated to its present site. The Kanjin-kō, which involved relocating the four {{ill|Shinrei|de|Shinrei|ja|神霊}} used for the autumn festival, saw two of these donated from Kurooka Village in {{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7 during {{ill|June|de|Juni|ja|6月}}, while the other two were contributed from Sasayama Town in {{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8 during {{ill|September|de|September|ja|9月}} (although there is also a theory placing it in Genroku 13). The nine floats, known as the “Chinchiki-yama,” were donated by various local communities from {{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 onward. They were modeled after the floats of Kyoto’s Gion Festival and are noted for their splendid, luxurious appearance. Until the late {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治}}, floats adorned with motifs such as phoenixes, peacocks, cycads, and shōjō were paraded; however, the advent of {{ill|Electric wires|de|Überlandleitungen|ja|電線}} eventually necessitated floats without such adornments. In addition, the {{ill|Taiko drum float|de|Taiko-Festwagen|ja|太鼓御興}} is said to have originated during the {{ill|Culture and Bunsei eras|de|Bunka/Bunsei|ja|文化・文政}}, when potters of {{ill|Ōjiyama ware|de|Ōjiyama-Keramik|ja|王地山焼}} first began carrying it. ;Hoko Mountain [[File:春日神社山鉾巡行PA160707.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street.]] :It measures 6 meters in length and is approximately 5 meters high; the height up to the {{ill|Roof|de|Dach|ja|屋根}} is about two-thirds that of the floats used in Kyoto’s Gion Festival, clearly reflecting Kyoto’s influence. The original Hoko Mountain was smaller and simpler than the current one; however, around the mid-{{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代}}, it evolved to its present scale and gained in splendor. It is said that the first Hoko Mountain was constructed in {{ill|1663|de|1663|ja|1663年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3) on Mikasa Mountain in Kamigawara Town. ({{ill|Tamba-Sasayama municipal designation|de|Städtisches Kulturgut Tamba-Sasayama|ja|篠山市指定文化財}}) *Upper Town – 4 units : Mikasa Mountain (Kamigawara Town), Phoenix Mountain (Shimogawara Town), Peacock Mountain (Kamitachi Town), Takasago Mountain (Shimotachi Town) *Lower Town – 5 units : Kenhoko Mountain (Gofukuchō), Shōshō Mountain (Kaminikaimachō), Isakotsu Mountain (Shimonikaimachō), Sotetsu Mountain (Uoyachō), Kagami Mountain (Nishimachō) ;Mikoshi :The mikoshi were donated and enshrined in {{ill|1694|de|1694|ja|1694年}} ({{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7) and {{ill|1779|de|1779|ja|1779年}} ({{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8), respectively. The presence of four mikoshi corresponds to the four festival deities of Kasuga Jinja – namely, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}}. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5890.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 Sasayama Castle Town DSCF6020.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF6006.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|Yamahoko Procession. In front of Kasuga Jinja. ==Shrine Buildings== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Okina Dedication (2006.1.1)]] :The Noh stage was built in {{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年}} (the first year of {{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久}}) during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期}} by Aoyama Tadayoshi, the 13th lord of the Sasayama domain. Known as a Noh enthusiast, he dedicated this stage as an offering. The architectural work was executed by Inayama Kasha and Nagai Rihei, while the pine paintings in the stage background were created by Matsuoka Sōemon; at the time, it was acclaimed as the finest Noh stage west of {{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根}}. To enhance the resonance of footsteps on the floorboards, a large jar was installed underneath—and its perfection is considered among the best in the nation. On {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} during New Year in Japan, the Okina dedication is held, and every year in early {{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月}}, when the {{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜}} bloom, the "Sasayama Kasuga Noh" is staged. In {{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年}} (Heisei 15), it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}.篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂}} :Constructed in {{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 27) August as a memorial to peace, the Ema Hall houses a horse painting that originally hung in the old prayer hall. In {{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安}} 2, Matsudaira Tadakuni (the lord of the Akashi Domain in Harima Province) dedicated a finely detailed Black Divine Horse by Kano Naonobu. Owing to a local legend that at night the horse would escape the painting to devour beans in the Kurooka fields, a metal net was installed exclusively on this ema. This Black Divine Horse has also been designated as a treasured cultural property. ==Cultural Properties== ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * Noh stage ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * Black Divine Horse painting ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} – Okina dedication; held shortly after midnight. * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} – Kasuga Noh. * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} – Autumn festival. ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR Fukuchiyama Line – “{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}” * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: Wing Kamiki (formerly Kamiki Green Bus) – a short walk from “In front of Kasuga Jinja” ==Notes== ==See also== * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (deity)|de|Kasuga (Gottheit)|ja|春日神}} ==External links== * {{Official website|http://kasuga.tanba-sasayama.net/|丹波篠山 春日神社}} * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O1fzbIBsys4 Kasuga Jinja Autumn Festival (Sasayama City) Yoimiya – 2008.10.18 ~ Tanba Shinposha] {{ill|Dead link|de|Toter Link|ja|リンク切れ}} | date=January 2020 * [https://tourism.sasayama.jp/association/ Tanba Sasayama Tourism Association Official Website] * [https://www.google.co.jp/maps?hl=ja&lr=um&um=1&ie=UTF-8&q=%E6%98%A5%E6%97%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&near=%E5%85%B5%E5%BA%AB%E7%9C%8C%E7%AF%A0%E5%B1%B1%E5%B8%82&fb=1&view=text&latlng=15104569364938192884 Kasuga Jinja Map] * [http://edu.city.sasayama.hyogo.jp/tiikibunka/kasuganou34/kasuganou34.html The 46th Kasuga Noh (Sasayama City, Hyōgo Prefecture, Social Education & Cultural Properties Division)] On Aoyama Street.]] :It measures 6 meters in length and is approximately 5 meters high; the height up to the {{ill|Roof|de|Dach|ja|屋根}} is about two-thirds that of the floats used in Kyoto’s Gion Festival, clearly reflecting Kyoto’s influence. It is said that the first Hoko Mountain was constructed in {{ill|1663|de|1663|ja|1663年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3) on Mikasa Mountain in Kamigawara Town. ({{ill|Tamba-Sasayama municipal designation|de|Städtisches Kulturgut Tamba-Sasayama|ja|篠山市指定文化財}}) *Upper Town – 4 units : Mikasa Mountain (Kamigawara Town), Phoenix Mountain (Shimogawara Town), Peacock Mountain (Kamitachi Town), Takasago Mountain (Shimotachi Town) *Lower Town – 5 units : Kenhoko Mountain (Gofukuchō), Shōshō Mountain (Kaminikaimachō), Isakotsu Mountain (Shimonikaimachō), Sotetsu Mountain (Uoyachō), Kagami Mountain (Nishimachō) ;Mikoshi :The mikoshi were donated and enshrined in {{ill|1694|de|1694|ja|1694年}} ({{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7) and {{ill|1779|de|1779|ja|1779年}} ({{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8), respectively. The presence of four mikoshi corresponds to the four festival deities of Kasuga Jinja – namely, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}}. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5890.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 Sasayama Castle Town DSCF6020.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF6006.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|Yamahoko Procession. In front of Kasuga Jinja. ==Shrine Buildings== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Okina Dedication (2006.1.1)]] :The Noh stage was built in {{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年}} (the first year of {{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久}}) during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期}} by Aoyama Tadayoshi, the 13th lord of the Sasayama domain. Known as a Noh enthusiast, he dedicated this stage as an offering. The architectural work was executed by Inayama Kasha and Nagai Rihei, while the pine paintings in the stage background were created by Matsuoka Sōemon; at the time, it was acclaimed as the finest Noh stage west of {{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根}}. To enhance the resonance of footsteps on the floorboards, a large jar was installed underneath—and its perfection is considered among the best in the nation. On {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} during New Year in Japan, the Okina dedication is held, and every year in early {{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月}}, when the {{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜}} bloom, the "Sasayama Kasuga Noh" is staged. In {{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年}} (Heisei 15), it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}.篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂}} :Constructed in {{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 27) August as a memorial to peace, the Ema Hall houses a horse painting that originally hung in the old prayer hall. In {{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安}} 2, Matsudaira Tadakuni (the lord of the Akashi Domain in Harima Province) dedicated a finely detailed Black Divine Horse by Kano Naonobu. Owing to a local legend that at night the horse would escape the painting to devour beans in the Kurooka fields, a metal net was installed exclusively on this ema. This Black Divine Horse has also been designated as a treasured cultural property. ==Cultural Properties== ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * Noh stage ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * Black Divine Horse painting ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} – Okina dedication; held shortly after midnight. * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} – Kasuga Noh. * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} – Autumn festival. ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR Fukuchiyama Line – “{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}” * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: Wing Kamiki (formerly Kamiki Green Bus) – a short walk from “In front of Kasuga Jinja” ==Notes== ==See also== * {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Jinja (春日神社) | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|04|41.7|N|135|13|07.8|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Jinja (Tamba-Sasayama)}} | enshrined_deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}
{{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|姫大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}} | main_hall = | main_festival = 3rd Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} | image = [[File:篠山春日神社本殿sasayama-kasugajinjaPA156109.jpg|200px|Main Hall]] | map = {{Location map | Japan Hyogo | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 04 | lat_sec = 41.7 | lon_deg = 135 | lon_min = 13 | lon_sec = 07.8 | label = Kasuga Jinja | position = center | width = 200 | float = center }} }} '''Kasuga Jinja''' (春日神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75. It was {{ill|Branch shrine|de|Zweigschrein|ja|分霊}} of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in {{ill|Nara|de|Nara|ja|奈良}}. Under the {{ill|Former shrine ranking system|de|Ehemaliges Shintō-Rangsystem|ja|旧社格}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}}. ==Overview== It enshrines a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} of Nara, and locally the deity is known as "Okasuga-san" – an {{ill|Ujigami|de|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}. The spring {{ill|Kasuga Noh|de|Kasuga Nō|ja|春日能}} performed on the {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} and the autumn {{ill|Festival|de|Fest|ja|祭礼}} held on the third Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} are said to be modeled after the {{ill|Gion Festival|de|Gion-Fest|ja|祇園祭}} of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|de|Yasaka-Schrein|ja|八坂神社|lt=Yasaka|lt_ja=八坂|lt_de=Yasaka}} in Kyoto. In this celebration, four mikoshi (portable shrines) and nine elaborately decorated yamahoko floats parade through an old town that retains the atmosphere of "Little Kyoto" with the resonant sounds of flutes ({{ill|Fue|de|Fue|ja|笛}}) and bells ({{ill|Kane (instrument)|de|Kane (Musikinstrument)|ja|鐘}}), evoking a charm regarded as more atmospheric than the modern rendition of Kyoto’s Gion Festival. ==History== In the early {{ill|Heian period|de|Heian-Zeit|ja|平安時代}}, in {{ill|876|de|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 18), Fujiwara no Mototsune and his son Fujiwara no Tokihira, then lords of Hioki Manor, enshrined a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} (located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県}}) – the guardian deity of the Fujiwara clan – in a small hill in the southern part of Kurooka Village (then known as Sasayama, where {{ill|Sasayama Castle|de|Burg Sasayama|ja|篠山城}} now stands). This is considered the origin of the shrine. Later, in {{ill|1609|de|1609|ja|1609年}} ({{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14), during the construction of Sasayama Castle, the shrine was relocated to its present site. The Kanjin-kō, which involved relocating the four {{ill|Shinrei|de|Shinrei|ja|神霊}} used for the autumn festival, saw two of these donated from Kurooka Village in {{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7 during {{ill|June|de|Juni|ja|6月}}, while the other two were contributed from Sasayama Town in {{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8 during {{ill|September|de|September|ja|9月}} (although there is also a theory placing it in Genroku 13). The nine floats, known as the “Chinchiki-yama,” were donated by various local communities from {{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 onward. They were modeled after the floats of Kyoto’s Gion Festival and are noted for their splendid, luxurious appearance. Until the late {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治}}, floats adorned with motifs such as phoenixes, peacocks, cycads, and shōjō were paraded; however, the advent of {{ill|Electric wires|de|Überlandleitungen|ja|電線}} eventually necessitated floats without such adornments. In addition, the {{ill|Taiko drum float|de|Taiko-Festwagen|ja|太鼓御興}} is said to have originated during the {{ill|Culture and Bunsei eras|de|Bunka/Bunsei|ja|文化・文政}}, when potters of {{ill|Ōjiyama ware|de|Ōjiyama-Keramik|ja|王地山焼}} first began carrying it. ;Hoko Mountain [[File:春日神社山鉾巡行PA160707.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street.]] :It measures 6 meters in length and is approximately 5 meters high; the height up to the {{ill|Roof|de|Dach|ja|屋根}} is about two-thirds that of the floats used in Kyoto’s Gion Festival, clearly reflecting Kyoto’s influence. The original Hoko Mountain was smaller and simpler than the current one; however, around the mid-{{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代}}, it evolved to its present scale and gained in splendor. It is said that the first Hoko Mountain was constructed in {{ill|1663|de|1663|ja|1663年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3) on Mikasa Mountain in Kamigawara Town. ({{ill|Tamba-Sasayama municipal designation|de|Städtisches Kulturgut Tamba-Sasayama|ja|篠山市指定文化財}}) *Upper Town – 4 units : Mikasa Mountain (Kamigawara Town), Phoenix Mountain (Shimogawara Town), Peacock Mountain (Kamitachi Town), Takasago Mountain (Shimotachi Town) *Lower Town – 5 units : Kenhoko Mountain (Gofukuchō), Shōshō Mountain (Kaminikaimachō), Isakotsu Mountain (Shimonikaimachō), Sotetsu Mountain (Uoyachō), Kagami Mountain (Nishimachō) ;Mikoshi :The mikoshi were donated and enshrined in {{ill|1694|de|1694|ja|1694年}} ({{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7) and {{ill|1779|de|1779|ja|1779年}} ({{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8), respectively. The presence of four mikoshi corresponds to the four festival deities of Kasuga Jinja – namely, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}}. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5890.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 Sasayama Castle Town DSCF6020.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF6006.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|Yamahoko Procession. In front of Kasuga Jinja. ==Shrine Buildings== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Okina Dedication (2006.1.1)]] :The Noh stage was built in {{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年}} (the first year of {{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久}}) during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期}} by Aoyama Tadayoshi, the 13th lord of the Sasayama domain. Known as a Noh enthusiast, he dedicated this stage as an offering. The architectural work was executed by Inayama Kasha and Nagai Rihei, while the pine paintings in the stage background were created by Matsuoka Sōemon; at the time, it was acclaimed as the finest Noh stage west of {{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根}}. To enhance the resonance of footsteps on the floorboards, a large jar was installed underneath—and its perfection is considered among the best in the nation. On {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} during New Year in Japan, the Okina dedication is held, and every year in early {{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月}}, when the {{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜}} bloom, the "Sasayama Kasuga Noh" is staged. In {{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年}} (Heisei 15), it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}.篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂}} :Constructed in {{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 27) August as a memorial to peace, the Ema Hall houses a horse painting that originally hung in the old prayer hall. In {{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安}} 2, Matsudaira Tadakuni (the lord of the Akashi Domain in Harima Province) dedicated a finely detailed Black Divine Horse by Kano Naonobu. Owing to a local legend that at night the horse would escape the painting to devour beans in the Kurooka fields, a metal net was installed exclusively on this ema. This Black Divine Horse has also been designated as a treasured cultural property. ==Cultural Properties== ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * Noh stage ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * Black Divine Horse painting ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} – Okina dedication; held shortly after midnight. * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} – Kasuga Noh. * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} – Autumn festival. ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR Fukuchiyama Line – “{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}” * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: Wing Kamiki (formerly Kamiki Green Bus) – a short walk from “In front of Kasuga Jinja” ==Notes== ==See also== * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主神}} * It was {{ill|Branch shrine|de|Zweigschrein|ja|分霊}} of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in {{ill|Nara|de|Nara|ja|奈良}}. Under the {{ill|Former shrine ranking system|de|Ehemaliges Shintō-Rangsystem|ja|旧社格}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}}. ==Overview== It enshrines a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} of Nara, and locally the deity is known as "Okasuga-san" – an {{ill|Ujigami|de|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}. The spring {{ill|Kasuga Noh|de|Kasuga Nō|ja|春日能}} performed on the {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} and the autumn {{ill|Festival|de|Fest|ja|祭礼}} held on the third Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} are said to be modeled after the {{ill|Gion Festival|de|Gion-Fest|ja|祇園祭}} of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|de|Yasaka-Schrein|ja|八坂神社|lt=Yasaka|lt_ja=八坂|lt_de=Yasaka}} in Kyoto. In this celebration, four mikoshi (portable shrines) and nine elaborately decorated yamahoko floats parade through an old town that retains the atmosphere of "Little Kyoto" with the resonant sounds of flutes ({{ill|Fue|de|Fue|ja|笛}}) and bells ({{ill|Kane (instrument)|de|Kane (Musikinstrument)|ja|鐘}}), evoking a charm regarded as more atmospheric than the modern rendition of Kyoto’s Gion Festival. ==History== In the early {{ill|Heian period|de|Heian-Zeit|ja|平安時代}}, in {{ill|876|de|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 18), Fujiwara no Mototsune and his son Fujiwara no Tokihira, then lords of Hioki Manor, enshrined a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} (located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県}}) – the guardian deity of the Fujiwara clan – in a small hill in the southern part of Kurooka Village (then known as Sasayama, where {{ill|Sasayama Castle|de|Burg Sasayama|ja|篠山城}} now stands). This is considered the origin of the shrine. Later, in {{ill|1609|de|1609|ja|1609年}} ({{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14), during the construction of Sasayama Castle, the shrine was relocated to its present site. The Kanjin-kō, which involved relocating the four {{ill|Shinrei|de|Shinrei|ja|神霊}} used for the autumn festival, saw two of these donated from Kurooka Village in {{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7 during {{ill|June|de|Juni|ja|6月}}, while the other two were contributed from Sasayama Town in {{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8 during {{ill|September|de|September|ja|9月}} (although there is also a theory placing it in Genroku 13). The nine floats, known as the “Chinchiki-yama,” were donated by various local communities from {{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 onward. They were modeled after the floats of Kyoto’s Gion Festival and are noted for their splendid, luxurious appearance. Until the late {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治}}, floats adorned with motifs such as phoenixes, peacocks, cycads, and shōjō were paraded; however, the advent of {{ill|Electric wires|de|Überlandleitungen|ja|電線}} eventually necessitated floats without such adornments. In addition, the {{ill|Taiko drum float|de|Taiko-Festwagen|ja|太鼓御興}} is said to have originated during the {{ill|Culture and Bunsei eras|de|Bunka/Bunsei|ja|文化・文政}}, when potters of {{ill|Ōjiyama ware|de|Ōjiyama-Keramik|ja|王地山焼}} first began carrying it. ;Hoko Mountain [[File:春日神社山鉾巡行PA160707.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street.]] :It measures 6 meters in length and is approximately 5 meters high; the height up to the {{ill|Roof|de|Dach|ja|屋根}} is about two-thirds that of the floats used in Kyoto’s Gion Festival, clearly reflecting Kyoto’s influence. The original Hoko Mountain was smaller and simpler than the current one; however, around the mid-{{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代}}, it evolved to its present scale and gained in splendor. It is said that the first Hoko Mountain was constructed in {{ill|1663|de|1663|ja|1663年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3) on Mikasa Mountain in Kamigawara Town. ({{ill|Tamba-Sasayama municipal designation|de|Städtisches Kulturgut Tamba-Sasayama|ja|篠山市指定文化財}}) *Upper Town – 4 units : Mikasa Mountain (Kamigawara Town), Phoenix Mountain (Shimogawara Town), Peacock Mountain (Kamitachi Town), Takasago Mountain (Shimotachi Town) *Lower Town – 5 units : Kenhoko Mountain (Gofukuchō), Shōshō Mountain (Kaminikaimachō), Isakotsu Mountain (Shimonikaimachō), Sotetsu Mountain (Uoyachō), Kagami Mountain (Nishimachō) ;Mikoshi :The mikoshi were donated and enshrined in {{ill|1694|de|1694|ja|1694年}} ({{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7) and {{ill|1779|de|1779|ja|1779年}} ({{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8), respectively. The presence of four mikoshi corresponds to the four festival deities of Kasuga Jinja – namely, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}}. In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF6006.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|Yamahoko Procession. In front of Kasuga Jinja. ==Shrine Buildings== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Okina Dedication (2006.1.1)]] :The Noh stage was built in {{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年}} (the first year of {{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久}}) during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期}} by Aoyama Tadayoshi, the 13th lord of the Sasayama domain. Known as a Noh enthusiast, he dedicated this stage as an offering. The architectural work was executed by Inayama Kasha and Nagai Rihei, while the pine paintings in the stage background were created by Matsuoka Sōemon; at the time, it was acclaimed as the finest Noh stage west of {{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根}}. To enhance the resonance of footsteps on the floorboards, a large jar was installed underneath—and its perfection is considered among the best in the nation. On {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} during New Year in Japan, the Okina dedication is held, and every year in early {{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月}}, when the {{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜}} bloom, the "Sasayama Kasuga Noh" is staged. In {{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年}} (Heisei 15), it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}.篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂}} :Constructed in {{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 27) August as a memorial to peace, the Ema Hall houses a horse painting that originally hung in the old prayer hall. In {{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安}} 2, Matsudaira Tadakuni (the lord of the Akashi Domain in Harima Province) dedicated a finely detailed Black Divine Horse by Kano Naonobu. Owing to a local legend that at night the horse would escape the painting to devour beans in the Kurooka fields, a metal net was installed exclusively on this ema. This Black Divine Horse has also been designated as a treasured cultural property. ==Cultural Properties== ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * Noh stage ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * Black Divine Horse painting ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} – Okina dedication; held shortly after midnight. * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} – Kasuga Noh. * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} – Autumn festival. ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR Fukuchiyama Line – “{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}” * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: Wing Kamiki (formerly Kamiki Green Bus) – a short walk from “In front of Kasuga Jinja” ==Notes== ==See also== * revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Jinja (春日神社) | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|04|41.7|N|135|13|07.8|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Jinja (Tamba-Sasayama)}} | enshrined_deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}
{{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|姫大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}} | main_hall = | main_festival = 3rd Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} | image = [[File:篠山春日神社本殿sasayama-kasugajinjaPA156109.jpg|200px|Main Hall]] | map = {{Location map | Japan Hyogo | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 04 | lat_sec = 41.7 | lon_deg = 135 | lon_min = 13 | lon_sec = 07.8 | label = Kasuga Jinja | position = center | width = 200 | float = center }} }} '''Kasuga Jinja''' (春日神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75. It was {{ill|Branch shrine|de|Zweigschrein|ja|分霊}} of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in {{ill|Nara|de|Nara|ja|奈良}}. Under the {{ill|Former shrine ranking system|de|Ehemaliges Shintō-Rangsystem|ja|旧社格}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}}. ==Overview== It enshrines a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} of Nara, and locally the deity is known as "Okasuga-san" – an {{ill|Ujigami|de|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}. The spring {{ill|Kasuga Noh|de|Kasuga Nō|ja|春日能}} performed on the {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} and the autumn {{ill|Festival|de|Fest|ja|祭礼}} held on the third Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} are said to be modeled after the {{ill|Gion Festival|de|Gion-Fest|ja|祇園祭}} of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|de|Yasaka-Schrein|ja|八坂神社|lt=Yasaka|lt_ja=八坂|lt_de=Yasaka}} in Kyoto. In this celebration, four mikoshi (portable shrines) and nine elaborately decorated yamahoko floats parade through an old town that retains the atmosphere of "Little Kyoto" with the resonant sounds of flutes ({{ill|Fue|de|Fue|ja|笛}}) and bells ({{ill|Kane (instrument)|de|Kane (Musikinstrument)|ja|鐘}}), evoking a charm regarded as more atmospheric than the modern rendition of Kyoto’s Gion Festival. ==History== In the early {{ill|Heian period|de|Heian-Zeit|ja|平安時代}}, in {{ill|876|de|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 18), Fujiwara no Mototsune and his son Fujiwara no Tokihira, then lords of Hioki Manor, enshrined a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} (located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県}}) – the guardian deity of the Fujiwara clan – in a small hill in the southern part of Kurooka Village (then known as Sasayama, where {{ill|Sasayama Castle|de|Burg Sasayama|ja|篠山城}} now stands). This is considered the origin of the shrine. Later, in {{ill|1609|de|1609|ja|1609年}} ({{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14), during the construction of Sasayama Castle, the shrine was relocated to its present site. The Kanjin-kō, which involved relocating the four {{ill|Shinrei|de|Shinrei|ja|神霊}} used for the autumn festival, saw two of these donated from Kurooka Village in {{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7 during {{ill|June|de|Juni|ja|6月}}, while the other two were contributed from Sasayama Town in {{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8 during {{ill|September|de|September|ja|9月}} (although there is also a theory placing it in Genroku 13). The nine floats, known as the “Chinchiki-yama,” were donated by various local communities from {{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 onward. They were modeled after the floats of Kyoto’s Gion Festival and are noted for their splendid, luxurious appearance. Until the late {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治}}, floats adorned with motifs such as phoenixes, peacocks, cycads, and shōjō were paraded; however, the advent of {{ill|Electric wires|de|Überlandleitungen|ja|電線}} eventually necessitated floats without such adornments. In addition, the {{ill|Taiko drum float|de|Taiko-Festwagen|ja|太鼓御興}} is said to have originated during the {{ill|Culture and Bunsei eras|de|Bunka/Bunsei|ja|文化・文政}}, when potters of {{ill|Ōjiyama ware|de|Ōjiyama-Keramik|ja|王地山焼}} first began carrying it. ;Hoko Mountain [[File:春日神社山鉾巡行PA160707.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street.]] :It measures 6 meters in length and is approximately 5 meters high; the height up to the {{ill|Roof|de|Dach|ja|屋根}} is about two-thirds that of the floats used in Kyoto’s Gion Festival, clearly reflecting Kyoto’s influence. The original Hoko Mountain was smaller and simpler than the current one; however, around the mid-{{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代}}, it evolved to its present scale and gained in splendor. It is said that the first Hoko Mountain was constructed in {{ill|1663|de|1663|ja|1663年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3) on Mikasa Mountain in Kamigawara Town. ({{ill|Tamba-Sasayama municipal designation|de|Städtisches Kulturgut Tamba-Sasayama|ja|篠山市指定文化財}}) *Upper Town – 4 units : Mikasa Mountain (Kamigawara Town), Phoenix Mountain (Shimogawara Town), Peacock Mountain (Kamitachi Town), Takasago Mountain (Shimotachi Town) *Lower Town – 5 units : Kenhoko Mountain (Gofukuchō), Shōshō Mountain (Kaminikaimachō), Isakotsu Mountain (Shimonikaimachō), Sotetsu Mountain (Uoyachō), Kagami Mountain (Nishimachō) ;Mikoshi :The mikoshi were donated and enshrined in {{ill|1694|de|1694|ja|1694年}} ({{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7) and {{ill|1779|de|1779|ja|1779年}} ({{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8), respectively. The presence of four mikoshi corresponds to the four festival deities of Kasuga Jinja – namely, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}}. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5890.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 Sasayama Castle Town DSCF6020.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF6006.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|Yamahoko Procession. In front of Kasuga Jinja. ==Shrine Buildings== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Okina Dedication (2006.1.1)]] :The Noh stage was built in {{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年}} (the first year of {{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久}}) during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期}} by Aoyama Tadayoshi, the 13th lord of the Sasayama domain. Known as a Noh enthusiast, he dedicated this stage as an offering. The architectural work was executed by Inayama Kasha and Nagai Rihei, while the pine paintings in the stage background were created by Matsuoka Sōemon; at the time, it was acclaimed as the finest Noh stage west of {{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根}}. To enhance the resonance of footsteps on the floorboards, a large jar was installed underneath—and its perfection is considered among the best in the nation. On {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} during New Year in Japan, the Okina dedication is held, and every year in early {{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月}}, when the {{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜}} bloom, the "Sasayama Kasuga Noh" is staged. In {{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年}} (Heisei 15), it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}.篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂}} :Constructed in {{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 27) August as a memorial to peace, the Ema Hall houses a horse painting that originally hung in the old prayer hall. In {{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安}} 2, Matsudaira Tadakuni (the lord of the Akashi Domain in Harima Province) dedicated a finely detailed Black Divine Horse by Kano Naonobu. Owing to a local legend that at night the horse would escape the painting to devour beans in the Kurooka fields, a metal net was installed exclusively on this ema. This Black Divine Horse has also been designated as a treasured cultural property. ==Cultural Properties== ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * Noh stage ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * Black Divine Horse painting ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} – Okina dedication; held shortly after midnight. * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} – Kasuga Noh. * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} – Autumn festival. ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR Fukuchiyama Line – “{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}” * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: Wing Kamiki (formerly Kamiki Green Bus) – a short walk from “In front of Kasuga Jinja” ==Notes== ==See also== * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主神}} * {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}} * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (deity)|de|Kasuga (Gottheit)|ja|春日神}} ==External links== * {{Official website|http://kasuga.tanba-sasayama.net/|丹波篠山 春日神社}} * [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O1fzbIBsys4 Kasuga Jinja Autumn Festival (Sasayama City) Yoimiya – 2008.10.18 ~ Tanba Shinposha] {{ill|Dead link|de|Toter Link|ja|リンク切れ}} | date=January 2020 * [https://tourism.sasayama.jp/association/ Tanba Sasayama Tourism Association Official Website] * [https://www.google.co.jp/maps?hl=ja&lr=um&um=1&ie=UTF-8&q=%E6%98%A5%E6%97%A5%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&near=%E5%85%B5%E5%BA%AB%E7%9C%8C%E7%AF%A0%E5%B1%B1%E5%B8%82&fb=1&view=text&latlng=15104569364938192884 Kasuga Jinja Map] * [http://edu.city.sasayama.hyogo.jp/tiikibunka/kasuganou34/kasuganou34.html The 46th Kasuga Noh (Sasayama City, Hyōgo Prefecture, Social Education & Cultural Properties Division)] File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5890.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 Sasayama Castle Town DSCF6020.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF6006.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|Yamahoko Procession. In front of Kasuga Jinja. ==Shrine Buildings== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Okina Dedication (2006.1.1)]] :The Noh stage was built in {{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年}} (the first year of {{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久}}) during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期}} by Aoyama Tadayoshi, the 13th lord of the Sasayama domain. Known as a Noh enthusiast, he dedicated this stage as an offering. The architectural work was executed by Inayama Kasha and Nagai Rihei, while the pine paintings in the stage background were created by Matsuoka Sōemon; at the time, it was acclaimed as the finest Noh stage west of {{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根}}. To enhance the resonance of footsteps on the floorboards, a large jar was installed underneath—and its perfection is considered among the best in the nation. On {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} during New Year in Japan, the Okina dedication is held, and every year in early {{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月}}, when the {{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜}} bloom, the "Sasayama Kasuga Noh" is staged. In {{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年}} (Heisei 15), it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}.篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂}} :Constructed in {{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 27) August as a memorial to peace, the Ema Hall houses a horse painting that originally hung in the old prayer hall. In {{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安}} 2, Matsudaira Tadakuni (the lord of the Akashi Domain in Harima Province) dedicated a finely detailed Black Divine Horse by Kano Naonobu. Owing to a local legend that at night the horse would escape the painting to devour beans in the Kurooka fields, a metal net was installed exclusively on this ema. This Black Divine Horse has also been designated as a treasured cultural property. ==Cultural Properties== ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * Noh stage ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * Black Divine Horse painting ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} – Okina dedication; held shortly after midnight. * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} – Kasuga Noh. * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} – Autumn festival. ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR Fukuchiyama Line – “{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}” * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: Wing Kamiki (formerly Kamiki Green Bus) – a short walk from “In front of Kasuga Jinja” ==Notes== ==See also== * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主神}} * revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Kasuga Jinja (春日神社) | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|04|41.7|N|135|13|07.8|E|scale:20000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kasuga Jinja (Tamba-Sasayama)}} | enshrined_deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}
{{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}
{{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|姫大神}} | shrine_rank = {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}} | main_hall = | main_festival = 3rd Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} | image = [[File:篠山春日神社本殿sasayama-kasugajinjaPA156109.jpg|200px|Main Hall]] | map = {{Location map | Japan Hyogo | lat_deg = 35 | lat_min = 04 | lat_sec = 41.7 | lon_deg = 135 | lon_min = 13 | lon_sec = 07.8 | label = Kasuga Jinja | position = center | width = 200 | float = center }} }} '''Kasuga Jinja''' (春日神社) is a Shinto shrine located at {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|de|Präfektur Hyōgo|ja|兵庫県}}{{ill|Tamba-Sasayama|de|Tamba-Sasayama|ja|丹波篠山市}}黒岡75. It was {{ill|Branch shrine|de|Zweigschrein|ja|分霊}} of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} in {{ill|Nara|de|Nara|ja|奈良}}. Under the {{ill|Former shrine ranking system|de|Ehemaliges Shintō-Rangsystem|ja|旧社格}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社}}. ==Overview== It enshrines a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} of Nara, and locally the deity is known as "Okasuga-san" – an {{ill|Ujigami|de|Ujigami|ja|氏神}}. The spring {{ill|Kasuga Noh|de|Kasuga Nō|ja|春日能}} performed on the {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} and the autumn {{ill|Festival|de|Fest|ja|祭礼}} held on the third Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月}} are said to be modeled after the {{ill|Gion Festival|de|Gion-Fest|ja|祇園祭}} of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|de|Yasaka-Schrein|ja|八坂神社|lt=Yasaka|lt_ja=八坂|lt_de=Yasaka}} in Kyoto. In this celebration, four mikoshi (portable shrines) and nine elaborately decorated yamahoko floats parade through an old town that retains the atmosphere of "Little Kyoto" with the resonant sounds of flutes ({{ill|Fue|de|Fue|ja|笛}}) and bells ({{ill|Kane (instrument)|de|Kane (Musikinstrument)|ja|鐘}}), evoking a charm regarded as more atmospheric than the modern rendition of Kyoto’s Gion Festival. ==History== In the early {{ill|Heian period|de|Heian-Zeit|ja|平安時代}}, in {{ill|876|de|876|ja|876年}} ({{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 18), Fujiwara no Mototsune and his son Fujiwara no Tokihira, then lords of Hioki Manor, enshrined a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社}} (located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県}}) – the guardian deity of the Fujiwara clan – in a small hill in the southern part of Kurooka Village (then known as Sasayama, where {{ill|Sasayama Castle|de|Burg Sasayama|ja|篠山城}} now stands). This is considered the origin of the shrine. Later, in {{ill|1609|de|1609|ja|1609年}} ({{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長}} 14), during the construction of Sasayama Castle, the shrine was relocated to its present site. The Kanjin-kō, which involved relocating the four {{ill|Shinrei|de|Shinrei|ja|神霊}} used for the autumn festival, saw two of these donated from Kurooka Village in {{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7 during {{ill|June|de|Juni|ja|6月}}, while the other two were contributed from Sasayama Town in {{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8 during {{ill|September|de|September|ja|9月}} (although there is also a theory placing it in Genroku 13). The nine floats, known as the “Chinchiki-yama,” were donated by various local communities from {{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 onward. They were modeled after the floats of Kyoto’s Gion Festival and are noted for their splendid, luxurious appearance. Until the late {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治}}, floats adorned with motifs such as phoenixes, peacocks, cycads, and shōjō were paraded; however, the advent of {{ill|Electric wires|de|Überlandleitungen|ja|電線}} eventually necessitated floats without such adornments. In addition, the {{ill|Taiko drum float|de|Taiko-Festwagen|ja|太鼓御興}} is said to have originated during the {{ill|Culture and Bunsei eras|de|Bunka/Bunsei|ja|文化・文政}}, when potters of {{ill|Ōjiyama ware|de|Ōjiyama-Keramik|ja|王地山焼}} first began carrying it. ;Hoko Mountain [[File:春日神社山鉾巡行PA160707.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street.]] :It measures 6 meters in length and is approximately 5 meters high; the height up to the {{ill|Roof|de|Dach|ja|屋根}} is about two-thirds that of the floats used in Kyoto’s Gion Festival, clearly reflecting Kyoto’s influence. The original Hoko Mountain was smaller and simpler than the current one; however, around the mid-{{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代}}, it evolved to its present scale and gained in splendor. It is said that the first Hoko Mountain was constructed in {{ill|1663|de|1663|ja|1663年}} ({{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3) on Mikasa Mountain in Kamigawara Town. ({{ill|Tamba-Sasayama municipal designation|de|Städtisches Kulturgut Tamba-Sasayama|ja|篠山市指定文化財}}) *Upper Town – 4 units : Mikasa Mountain (Kamigawara Town), Phoenix Mountain (Shimogawara Town), Peacock Mountain (Kamitachi Town), Takasago Mountain (Shimotachi Town) *Lower Town – 5 units : Kenhoko Mountain (Gofukuchō), Shōshō Mountain (Kaminikaimachō), Isakotsu Mountain (Shimonikaimachō), Sotetsu Mountain (Uoyachō), Kagami Mountain (Nishimachō) ;Mikoshi :The mikoshi were donated and enshrined in {{ill|1694|de|1694|ja|1694年}} ({{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 7) and {{ill|1779|de|1779|ja|1779年}} ({{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永}} 8), respectively. The presence of four mikoshi corresponds to the four festival deities of Kasuga Jinja – namely, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命}}, {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命}}, and {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神}}. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5890.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 Sasayama Castle Town DSCF6020.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF6006.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|Yamahoko Procession. In front of Kasuga Jinja. ==Shrine Buildings== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Okina Dedication (2006.1.1)]] :The Noh stage was built in {{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年}} (the first year of {{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久}}) during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期}} by Aoyama Tadayoshi, the 13th lord of the Sasayama domain. Known as a Noh enthusiast, he dedicated this stage as an offering. The architectural work was executed by Inayama Kasha and Nagai Rihei, while the pine paintings in the stage background were created by Matsuoka Sōemon; at the time, it was acclaimed as the finest Noh stage west of {{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根}}. To enhance the resonance of footsteps on the floorboards, a large jar was installed underneath—and its perfection is considered among the best in the nation. On {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} during New Year in Japan, the Okina dedication is held, and every year in early {{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月}}, when the {{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜}} bloom, the "Sasayama Kasuga Noh" is staged. In {{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年}} (Heisei 15), it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}}.篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂}} :Constructed in {{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年}} ({{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 27) August as a memorial to peace, the Ema Hall houses a horse painting that originally hung in the old prayer hall. In {{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安}} 2, Matsudaira Tadakuni (the lord of the Akashi Domain in Harima Province) dedicated a finely detailed Black Divine Horse by Kano Naonobu. Owing to a local legend that at night the horse would escape the painting to devour beans in the Kurooka fields, a metal net was installed exclusively on this ema. This Black Divine Horse has also been designated as a treasured cultural property. ==Cultural Properties== ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * Noh stage ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * Black Divine Horse painting ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日}} – Okina dedication; held shortly after midnight. * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬}} – Kasuga Noh. * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日}} – Autumn festival. ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道}}: JR Fukuchiyama Line – “{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅}}” * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス}}: Wing Kamiki (formerly Kamiki Green Bus) – a short walk from “In front of Kasuga Jinja” ==Notes== ==See also== * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|タケミカヅチ}} * {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主神}} * It was {{ill|Branch shrine|de|Zweigschrein|ja|分霊|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nara|de|Nara|ja|奈良|12=draft}}. Under the {{ill|Former shrine ranking system|de|Ehemaliges Shintō-Rangsystem|ja|旧社格|12=draft}}, it held the status of a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|de|Präfekturschrein|ja|県社|12=draft}}. ==Overview== It enshrines a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社|12=draft}} of Nara, and locally the deity is known as "Okasuga-san" – an {{ill|Ujigami|de|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}}. The spring {{ill|Kasuga Noh|de|Kasuga Nō|ja|春日能|12=draft}} performed on the {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿|12=draft}} and the autumn {{ill|Festival|de|Fest|ja|祭礼|12=draft}} held on the third Saturday and Sunday of {{ill|October|de|Oktober|ja|10月|12=draft}} are said to be modeled after the {{ill|Gion Festival|de|Gion-Fest|ja|祇園祭|12=draft}} of {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|de|Yasaka-Schrein|ja|八坂神社|lt=Yasaka|lt_ja=八坂|lt_de=Yasaka|12=draft}} in Kyoto. In this celebration, four mikoshi (portable shrines) and nine elaborately decorated yamahoko floats parade through an old town that retains the atmosphere of "Little Kyoto" with the resonant sounds of flutes ({{ill|Fue|de|Fue|ja|笛|12=draft}}) and bells ({{ill|Kane (instrument)|de|Kane (Musikinstrument)|ja|鐘|12=draft}}), evoking a charm regarded as more atmospheric than the modern rendition of Kyoto’s Gion Festival. ==History== In the early {{ill|Heian period|de|Heian-Zeit|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, in {{ill|876|de|876|ja|876年|12=draft}} ({{ill|Jōgan|de|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 18), Fujiwara no Mototsune and his son Fujiwara no Tokihira, then lords of Hioki Manor, enshrined a branch of the {{ill|Kasuga Grand Shrine|de|Kasuga-Taisha|ja|春日大社|12=draft}} (located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|de|Präfektur Nara|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}) – the guardian deity of the Fujiwara clan – in a small hill in the southern part of Kurooka Village (then known as Sasayama, where {{ill|Sasayama Castle|de|Burg Sasayama|ja|篠山城|12=draft}} now stands). This is considered the origin of the shrine. Later, in {{ill|1609|de|1609|ja|1609年|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keichō|de|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft}} 14), during the construction of Sasayama Castle, the shrine was relocated to its present site. The Kanjin-kō, which involved relocating the four {{ill|Shinrei|de|Shinrei|ja|神霊|12=draft}} used for the autumn festival, saw two of these donated from Kurooka Village in {{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 7 during {{ill|June|de|Juni|ja|6月|12=draft}}, while the other two were contributed from Sasayama Town in {{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永|12=draft}} 8 during {{ill|September|de|September|ja|9月|12=draft}} (although there is also a theory placing it in Genroku 13). The nine floats, known as the “Chinchiki-yama,” were donated by various local communities from {{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 3 onward. They were modeled after the floats of Kyoto’s Gion Festival and are noted for their splendid, luxurious appearance. Until the late {{ill|Meiji period|de|Meiji-Zeit|ja|明治|12=draft}}, floats adorned with motifs such as phoenixes, peacocks, cycads, and shōjō were paraded; however, the advent of {{ill|Electric wires|de|Überlandleitungen|ja|電線|12=draft}} eventually necessitated floats without such adornments. In addition, the {{ill|Taiko drum float|de|Taiko-Festwagen|ja|太鼓御興|12=draft}} is said to have originated during the {{ill|Culture and Bunsei eras|de|Bunka/Bunsei|ja|文化・文政|12=draft}}, when potters of {{ill|Ōjiyama ware|de|Ōjiyama-Keramik|ja|王地山焼|12=draft}} first began carrying it. ;Hoko Mountain [[File:春日神社山鉾巡行PA160707.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street.]] :It measures 6 meters in length and is approximately 5 meters high; the height up to the {{ill|Roof|de|Dach|ja|屋根|12=draft}} is about two-thirds that of the floats used in Kyoto’s Gion Festival, clearly reflecting Kyoto’s influence. The original Hoko Mountain was smaller and simpler than the current one; however, around the mid-{{ill|Edo period|de|Edo-Zeit|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, it evolved to its present scale and gained in splendor. It is said that the first Hoko Mountain was constructed in {{ill|1663|de|1663|ja|1663年|12=draft}} ({{ill|Kanbun|de|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 3) on Mikasa Mountain in Kamigawara Town. ({{ill|Tamba-Sasayama municipal designation|de|Städtisches Kulturgut Tamba-Sasayama|ja|篠山市指定文化財|12=draft}}) *Upper Town – 4 units : Mikasa Mountain (Kamigawara Town), Phoenix Mountain (Shimogawara Town), Peacock Mountain (Kamitachi Town), Takasago Mountain (Shimotachi Town) *Lower Town – 5 units : Kenhoko Mountain (Gofukuchō), Shōshō Mountain (Kaminikaimachō), Isakotsu Mountain (Shimonikaimachō), Sotetsu Mountain (Uoyachō), Kagami Mountain (Nishimachō) ;Mikoshi :The mikoshi were donated and enshrined in {{ill|1694|de|1694|ja|1694年|12=draft}} ({{ill|Genroku|de|Genroku|ja|元禄|12=draft}} 7) and {{ill|1779|de|1779|ja|1779年|12=draft}} ({{ill|An'ei|de|An’ei|ja|安永|12=draft}} 8), respectively. The presence of four mikoshi corresponds to the four festival deities of Kasuga Jinja – namely, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|武甕槌命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Futsunushi-no-kami|de|Futsunushi-no-kami|ja|経津主命|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Hime-Ōkami|de|Hime-Ōkami|ja|比売神|12=draft}}. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5890.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Isakotsu Mountain). On Aoyama Street. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 Sasayama Castle Town DSCF6020.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-15 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF6006.jpg|Yamahoko Procession (Phoenix Mountain). In Nikaimachi. File:2023-10-14 春日神社山鉾巡行 DSCF5989.jpg|Yamahoko Procession. In front of Kasuga Jinja. ==Shrine Buildings== * {{ill|Main Hall|de|Haupthalle (Shintō)|ja|本殿|12=draft}} * {{ill|Noh stage|de|Nō-Bühne|ja|能楽殿|12=draft}} [[File:春日神社ー篠山ー翁奉納P1011774.jpg|thumbnail|180px|Okina Dedication (2006.1.1)]] :The Noh stage was built in {{ill|1861|de|1861|ja|1861年|12=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Bunkyū|de|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft}}) during the {{ill|Bakumatsu|de|Bakumatsu|ja|江戸時代末期|12=draft}} by Aoyama Tadayoshi, the 13th lord of the Sasayama domain. Known as a Noh enthusiast, he dedicated this stage as an offering. The architectural work was executed by Inayama Kasha and Nagai Rihei, while the pine paintings in the stage background were created by Matsuoka Sōemon; at the time, it was acclaimed as the finest Noh stage west of {{ill|Hakone|de|Hakone|ja|箱根|12=draft}}. To enhance the resonance of footsteps on the floorboards, a large jar was installed underneath—and its perfection is considered among the best in the nation. On {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日|12=draft}} during New Year in Japan, the Okina dedication is held, and every year in early {{ill|April|de|April|ja|4月|12=draft}}, when the {{ill|Sakura|de|Sakura|ja|桜|12=draft}} bloom, the "Sasayama Kasuga Noh" is staged. In {{ill|2003|de|2003|ja|2003年|12=draft}} (Heisei 15), it was designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|de|Wichtiges Kulturgut (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}}.篠山市教育委員会 現地説明看板 * {{ill|Ema Hall|de|Ema-Halle|ja|絵馬堂|12=draft}} :Constructed in {{ill|1952|de|1952|ja|1952年|12=draft}} ({{ill|Shōwa|de|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 27) August as a memorial to peace, the Ema Hall houses a horse painting that originally hung in the old prayer hall. In {{ill|Keian|de|Keian|ja|慶安|12=draft}} 2, Matsudaira Tadakuni (the lord of the Akashi Domain in Harima Province) dedicated a finely detailed Black Divine Horse by Kano Naonobu. Owing to a local legend that at night the horse would escape the painting to devour beans in the Kurooka fields, a metal net was installed exclusively on this ema. This Black Divine Horse has also been designated as a treasured cultural property. ==Cultural Properties== ===Important Cultural Property (National)=== * Noh stage ===Tamba-Sasayama Municipal Cultural Properties=== * Black Divine Horse painting ==Annual Events== * {{ill|January 1|de|1. Januar|ja|1月1日|12=draft}} – Okina dedication; held shortly after midnight. * {{ill|Early April|de|Aprilanfang|ja|4月上旬|12=draft}} – Kasuga Noh. * {{ill|Third weekend of October|de|Drittes Oktoberwochenende|ja|10月第3土・日|12=draft}} – Autumn festival. ==Access== * {{ill|Rail transport in Japan|de|Schienenverkehr in Japan|ja|鉄道|12=draft}}: JR Fukuchiyama Line – “{{ill|Sasayamaguchi Station|de|Bahnhof Sasayamaguchi|ja|篠山口駅|12=draft}}” * {{ill|Bus transport in Japan|de|Busverkehr in Japan|ja|バス|12=draft}}: Wing Kamiki (formerly Kamiki Green Bus) – a short walk from “In front of Kasuga Jinja” ==Notes== ==See also== * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|de|Ame-no-Koyane-no-mikoto|ja|天児屋命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|de|Takemikazuchi-no-mikoto|ja|タケミカヅチ|12=draft}} *
